pi_items.source: Stanford King Institute, Wikipedia, King Center, multiple academic sources confirm this as the correct historical title
Historical document metadata from Stanford King Institute and UT Austin archive confirm author is Martin Luther King Jr., last name King
Multiple sources including academic archives confirm the letter was addressed to Eight Alabama Clergymen in response to their 'Call for Unity' statement
UT Austin archive PDF header (1963_mlk_letter.pdf), Stanford King Institute, letterfromjail.com, and multiple educational sources confirm date as April 16, 1963
Multiple sources including Facing History & Ourselves, Shmoop study guides, and Britannica-level educational resources confirm this is an open letter

letterfromjail.com provides full text showing the letter extensively discusses nonviolent resistance; Stanford King Institute and multiple educational sources confirm this is central to the letter's argument

privileged_info_summary: The task requires adding a citation for Martin Luther King Jr.'s 'Letter from Birmingham Jail,' a real historical document dated April 16, 1963. The letter is an open letter addressed to Eight Alabama Clergymen in response to their criticism of King's nonviolent civil disobedience campaign in Birmingham. All metadata claims in the task are factually accurate and verified against authoritative sources including the Stanford King Institute, UT Austin archival materials, and educational institutions. The letter extensively discusses nonviolent resistance as a moral and practical strategy for civil rights advancement.
